agent_output: {"executive_summary":"Integration architecture for a logistics platform connecting three external systems (SAP ERP via SFTP/XML, DHL Carrier via REST/webhooks, Analytics via Kafka) using an Anti-Corruption Layer per external system, an internal event backbone on Kafka, and dedicated adapter components that translate between external protocols/formats and the platform's canonical domain events. The design prioritizes loose coupling, independent evolvability of each integration, and operational resilience through idempotent processing and dead-letter handling.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Internal Event Bus (Kafka)","responsibility":"Canonical backbone for all domain events (OrderPlaced, OrderConfirmed, ShipmentCreated, DeliveryAttempted, DeliveryCompleted, DeliveryFailed). Single source of truth for event flow. All internal services publish and subscribe here.","boundaries":"Owns the canonical event schema (Avro/Protobuf with a Schema Registry). No external system format leaks into these topics."},{"name":"SAP ERP Adapter (System A)","responsibility":"Polls SFTP endpoint every 15 minutes, retrieves XML files, parses and validates them, translates confirmed orders into internal OrderConfirmed events, and publishes to the Internal Event Bus. Also consumes OrderPlaced events and generates outbound XML files for SAP if bidirectional sync is needed.","boundaries":"Owns the SFTP connection, XML parsing, and SAP-specific data mapping. Contains all SAP schema knowledge. Nothing outside this adapter knows about XML or SFTP."},{"name":"DHL Carrier Adapter (System B)","responsibility":"Exposes a webhook endpoint for DHL shipment status callbacks (DeliveryAttempted, DeliveryCompleted, DeliveryFailed). Also provides an outbound REST client to create shipments with DHL (translates ShipmentCreated intent into DHL API calls). Webhook endpoint must acknowledge within 500ms.","boundaries":"Owns DHL API contract knowledge, authentication, rate limiting, and retry logic. Translates DHL-specific status codes into canonical domain events."},{"name":"Analytics Stream Adapter (System C)","responsibility":"Consumes all domain events from the Internal Event Bus and forwards them to the Analytics Platform's Kafka cluster (or a dedicated set of topics shaped for analytics consumption). Handles schema transformation if the analytics platform expects a different event shape.","boundaries":"Owns the mapping from internal canonical events to the analytics-expected format. Acts as a Kafka-to-Kafka bridge with optional enrichment or filtering."},{"name":"Webhook Gateway","responsibility":"Lightweight HTTP reverse proxy in front of the DHL Carrier Adapter. Receives inbound webhooks, performs signature verification, writes the raw payload to a fast-ingest Kafka topic (dhl.webhooks.raw), and returns 200 immediately. This decouples HTTP response time from processing time, guaranteeing the 500ms SLA.","boundaries":"No business logic. Only authentication, payload capture, and fast acknowledgment."},{"name":"Outbox/Scheduler Service","responsibility":"Manages the SFTP polling schedule for SAP (cron-triggered every 15 min) and handles the transactional outbox pattern for outbound integrations to ensure exactly-once delivery semantics between the internal database and the event bus.","boundaries":"Owns scheduling and delivery guarantees. Does not own business logic."},{"name":"Dead Letter Queue (DLQ) Handler","responsibility":"Captures events that fail processing in any adapter after exhausting retries. Provides alerting, manual replay, and inspection tooling.","boundaries":"Cross-cutting operational concern. Each adapter routes its failures here."}],"interfaces":[{"from":"SAP ERP Adapter","to":"Internal Event Bus (Kafka)","contract":"Publishes OrderConfirmed events in canonical Avro schema to topic 'orders.confirmed'","coupling_type":"asynchronous, event-driven, loose"},{"from":"Internal Event Bus (Kafka)","to":"SAP ERP Adapter","contract":"Adapter subscribes to 'orders.placed' topic if bidirectional sync is required; generates XML and pushes to SFTP","coupling_type":"asynchronous, event-driven, loose"},{"from":"Webhook Gateway","to":"DHL Carrier Adapter","contract":"Raw DHL webhook payloads published to 'dhl.webhooks.raw' Kafka topic; adapter consumes, validates, and translates","coupling_type":"asynchronous, event-driven, loose"},{"from":"DHL Carrier Adapter","to":"Internal Event Bus (Kafka)","contract":"Publishes DeliveryAttempted, DeliveryCompleted, DeliveryFailed events in canonical schema","coupling_type":"asynchronous, event-driven, loose"},{"from":"Internal Event Bus (Kafka)","to":"DHL Carrier Adapter","contract":"Adapter consumes ShipmentCreated events and makes outbound REST calls to DHL's Create Shipment API","coupling_type":"asynchronous consumption triggers synchronous outbound call"},{"from":"Internal Event Bus (Kafka)","to":"Analytics Stream Adapter","contract":"Adapter consumes ALL domain event topics (orders.*, shipments.*, deliveries.*) and produces to analytics-bound topics","coupling_type":"asynchronous, event-driven, loose"},{"from":"Analytics Stream Adapter","to":"Analytics Platform (Kafka)","contract":"Produces transformed events to external Kafka cluster topics per analytics schema contract","coupling_type":"asynchronous, schema-coupled to analytics team's published language"},{"from":"All Adapters","to":"Dead Letter Queue (DLQ) Handler","contract":"Failed events routed to 'dlq.<adapter-name>' topics after retry exhaustion, with original event, error metadata, and attempt count","coupling_type":"asynchronous, operational"}],"data_flow":"1. OrderPlaced originates in the core platform → published to Internal Event Bus. 2. SAP Adapter polls SFTP every 15min → retrieves XML → translates to OrderConfirmed → publishes to bus. 3. Core platform consumes OrderConfirmed → creates shipment → publishes ShipmentCreated. 4. DHL Adapter consumes ShipmentCreated → calls DHL REST API to create shipment → stores tracking ID. 5. DHL sends webhook callbacks → Webhook Gateway acknowledges in <500ms and writes raw payload to Kafka → DHL Adapter consumes raw payload → translates to DeliveryAttempted/DeliveryCompleted/DeliveryFailed → publishes to bus. 6. Analytics Adapter continuously consumes ALL events from bus → transforms → produces to external Analytics Kafka cluster in real-time. 7. Any processing failure after retries → DLQ for manual intervention.","patterns_used":[{"pattern":"Anti-Corruption Layer (ACL)","rationale":"Each external system adapter acts as an ACL, preventing SAP XML schemas, DHL API contracts, and analytics event shapes from polluting the internal canonical domain model. External systems evolve independently; only the relevant adapter changes."},{"pattern":"Event-Driven Architecture / Event Backbone","rationale":"Kafka as the internal event bus decouples all components temporally and spatially. Producers and consumers evolve independently. Enables replay, auditing, and adding new consumers without modifying existing ones."},{"pattern":"Webhook Gateway with Fast-Ingest Queue","rationale":"Decouples the 500ms webhook SLA from actual business processing. The gateway writes raw payloads to Kafka and responds immediately, ensuring DHL never times out regardless of downstream processing latency."},{"pattern":"Transactional Outbox","rationale":"Ensures that database state changes and event publications are atomic. Prevents lost events or ghost events when the platform crashes between committing a transaction and publishing an event."},{"pattern":"Dead Letter Queue","rationale":"Provides operational resilience. Failed events are not lost; they accumulate in DLQ topics with full context for debugging and manual replay."},{"pattern":"Schema Registry (Avro/Protobuf)","rationale":"Enforces a canonical event contract across all producers and consumers. Schema evolution rules (backward/forward compatibility) prevent breaking changes from propagating."},{"pattern":"Adapter Pattern","rationale":"Each external system integration is encapsulated in a dedicated adapter with a clear interface, making integrations independently deployable, testable, and replaceable."},{"pattern":"Polling Consumer (SAP)","rationale":"SAP delivers files via SFTP on a schedule. A polling consumer with idempotent file processing (tracking processed filenames/checksums) is the natural fit for batch file-based integration."}]},"domain_model":{"bounded_contexts":[{"name":"Order Management","classification":"core","aggregates":["Order (root: OrderId, states: Placed → Confirmed → Shipped → Delivered/Failed)"]},{"name":"Shipment Management","classification":"core","aggregates":["Shipment (root: ShipmentId, linked to OrderId, states: Created → InTransit → Delivered/Failed, contains carrier tracking reference)"]},{"name":"ERP Integration","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["ERPSyncRecord (tracks processed SFTP files, maps SAP order IDs to internal OrderIds, idempotency keys)"]},{"name":"Carrier Integration","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["CarrierRequest (tracks outbound shipment creation calls, webhook correlation IDs, delivery attempt history)"]},{"name":"Analytics Integration","classification":"generic","aggregates":["No domain aggregates — stateless stream transformation only"]}],"context_map":[{"upstream":"Order Management","downstream":"ERP Integration","relationship":"Customer-Supplier: Order Management defines the OrderConfirmed event schema; ERP Integration adapter conforms to it and translates from SAP XML."},{"upstream":"Shipment Management","downstream":"Carrier Integration","relationship":"Customer-Supplier: Shipment Management defines ShipmentCreated; Carrier Integration conforms and translates to/from DHL API."},{"upstream":"Order Management + Shipment Management","downstream":"Analytics Integration","relationship":"Conformist: Analytics adapter consumes canonical events as-is and reshapes for the external analytics platform. No influence on upstream schemas."},{"upstream":"SAP ERP (External)","downstream":"ERP Integration","relationship":"Anti-Corruption Layer: ERP Integration translates SAP's XML schema into the internal canonical model, shielding the core domain."},{"upstream":"DHL API (External)","downstream":"Carrier Integration","relationship":"Anti-Corruption Layer: Carrier Integration translates DHL's REST/webhook contracts into canonical domain events."}]},"trade_offs":[{"decision":"Kafka as the internal event backbone (rather than a simpler message broker like RabbitMQ)","gains":"Log-based persistence enables replay and reprocessing. Consumer groups allow independent scaling. Natural fit for the analytics real-time stream requirement. Supports exactly-once semantics with transactions.","sacrifices":"Higher operational complexity. Requires Kafka cluster management, Schema Registry, and topic governance. Overkill if event volume is low.","alternatives_considered":["RabbitMQ with AMQP — simpler to operate but loses replay capability and is a less natural fit for the Kafka-based analytics requirement","Direct point-to-point REST calls between services — tighter coupling, no replay, harder to add new consumers"]},{"decision":"Webhook Gateway as a separate component with fast-ingest Kafka topic","gains":"Guarantees 500ms SLA independent of business logic complexity. Raw payloads are durably stored before processing, so no webhook data is lost even if the DHL adapter is down.","sacrifices":"Additional component to deploy and monitor. Slight increase in end-to-end latency for delivery status updates (gateway → Kafka → adapter → bus). Extra Kafka topic to manage.","alternatives_considered":["Process webhook inline in the adapter — simpler but risks breaching 500ms SLA under load or during downstream issues","Queue in Redis before processing — faster but less durable than Kafka; risks data loss on Redis failure"]},{"decision":"One adapter per external system (rather than a unified integration hub)","gains":"Each adapter is independently deployable, testable, and scalable. Failure in one integration does not affect others. Teams can own individual adapters.","sacrifices":"Some code duplication across adapters (retry logic, DLQ routing, health checks). More deployment units to manage.","alternatives_considered":["Unified ESB/integration hub — single deployment but becomes a monolithic bottleneck and single point of failure","Shared integration library — reduces duplication but creates coupling between adapter deployment cycles"]},{"decision":"Transactional Outbox over direct Kafka publish","gains":"Guarantees consistency between database state and published events. No dual-write problem.","sacrifices":"Requires an outbox table, a polling/CDC mechanism to relay events, and slightly higher publish latency.","alternatives_considered":["Direct publish to Kafka after DB commit — simpler but risks lost events on crash between commit and publish","Change Data Capture (Debezium) on core tables — powerful but adds infrastructure dependency and leaks DB schema into event contracts"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Adding new external system integrations","evolution_path":"New adapters can be added by subscribing to the internal event bus and implementing a new ACL. No changes to existing components. For example, adding a FedEx carrier is a new adapter consuming ShipmentCreated events."},{"area":"Replacing a carrier","evolution_path":"Swap the DHL Carrier Adapter for another carrier's adapter. Internal event contracts remain unchanged. Can run both adapters in parallel during migration using a routing layer."},{"area":"Analytics event enrichment","evolution_path":"The Analytics Stream Adapter can evolve independently — add joins with reference data, filtering, or aggregation — without touching the core domain or other adapters."},{"area":"Event schema evolution","evolution_path":"Schema Registry with backward-compatible evolution rules allows adding fields to domain events without breaking existing consumers. New consumers get richer data; old consumers ignore new fields."},{"area":"Scaling individual integrations","evolution_path":"Each adapter is an independent service with its own Kafka consumer group. The DHL adapter can scale horizontally to handle webhook spikes without affecting SAP or analytics processing."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Canonical event schema","reason":"All components couple to the canonical event definitions (Avro/Protobuf). Changing an event's core structure (renaming fields, changing semantics) requires coordinated updates across producers and consumers. This rigidity is intentional — the canonical model is the system's shared language and must be stable."},{"area":"Kafka as infrastructure","reason":"Deep dependency on Kafka for event backbone, webhook fast-ingest, and analytics streaming. Replacing Kafka would require significant rearchitecture. This is acceptable because Kafka is a proven, scalable technology for this exact use case."},{"area":"SFTP polling interval (15 min)","reason":"Dictated by SAP's file delivery schedule. Cannot get faster order confirmations without SAP changing their integration approach. This is an external constraint, not a design choice."}],"risks":[{"risk":"Webhook Gateway failure causes DHL to stop sending callbacks or mark endpoint as unhealthy","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Deploy Webhook Gateway behind a load balancer with multiple replicas. Implement health checks. DHL typically retries failed webhooks — ensure idempotent processing on our side. Monitor gateway response times with P99 alerting at 400ms."},{"risk":"SAP SFTP file processing fails silently, causing missed OrderConfirmed events","severity":"high","mitigation":"Track processed files by name and checksum in ERPSyncRecord aggregate. Alert if no files received within expected 15-min window (staleness check). Implement file-level idempotency to allow safe reprocessing."},{"risk":"Schema evolution breaks downstream consumers","severity":"high","mitigation":"Enforce backward-compatible schema evolution in Schema Registry. Run compatibility checks in CI/CD pipeline before deploying schema changes. Version topics if breaking changes are unavoidable."},{"risk":"DHL adapter outbound REST calls fail (DHL API downtime), causing ShipmentCreated events to pile up","severity":"high","mitigation":"Implement exponential backoff with circuit breaker on outbound DHL calls. Kafka consumer lag monitoring triggers alerts. DLQ for events that exceed retry budget. Manual replay tooling for recovery."},{"risk":"Kafka consumer lag in Analytics Adapter causes 'real-time' stream to fall behind","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Monitor consumer group lag. Auto-scale Analytics Adapter consumer instances. Set alerting thresholds for acceptable lag (e.g., <30 seconds). If analytics platform Kafka is slow, buffer locally."},{"risk":"Dual-write inconsistency if Transactional Outbox relay fails","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Use a battle-tested outbox relay (Debezium CDC or polling-based). Monitor relay lag. Ensure outbox table is cleaned up periodically to prevent unbounded growth."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Implement each adapter as an independent microservice (or at minimum, independently deployable module). Start with the Webhook Gateway and DHL Adapter first — the 500ms SLA is the tightest constraint and must be validated early. Then implement the SAP Adapter (lower risk due to batch nature), and finally the Analytics Adapter (consumer-only, lowest risk). Use Kafka with Schema Registry from day one — retrofitting schema governance is painful. Use the Transactional Outbox pattern in the core platform services that originate domain events.","files_to_create_or_modify":["services/webhook-gateway/ — Lightweight HTTP service (e.g., Spring Boot / Fastify / Go), receives POST, writes to Kafka, returns 200","services/dhl-adapter/ — Consumes dhl.webhooks.raw and orders.shipment-created topics, calls DHL REST API, publishes delivery events","services/sap-adapter/ — Cron-triggered SFTP poller, XML parser, publishes OrderConfirmed events","services/analytics-adapter/ — Kafka Streams or simple consumer-producer bridge to external analytics Kafka","schemas/ — Avro/Protobuf definitions for all canonical domain events, registered in Schema Registry","infra/kafka/ — Topic definitions, partition strategies, retention policies, consumer group configs","libs/integration-common/ — Shared retry, circuit breaker, DLQ routing, and health check utilities"],"patterns_to_follow":"Each adapter follows the same internal structure: Inbound Port (Kafka consumer or HTTP endpoint) → ACL Translator (maps external format to/from canonical) → Outbound Port (Kafka producer or external API client). Use hexagonal architecture within each adapter. Idempotency keys on all event processing. Circuit breakers on all outbound HTTP calls. Structured logging with correlation IDs across the entire event flow.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["Do not let SAP XML schema types leak into canonical events — always translate through the ACL","Do not process DHL webhooks synchronously in the HTTP handler — always persist first, process second","Do not share a Kafka consumer group across adapters — each adapter needs independent consumption","Do not skip the Schema Registry — ad-hoc JSON events become ungovernable quickly","Do not implement retry logic differently in each adapter — extract to a shared library","Do not forget idempotency — DHL may send duplicate webhooks, SFTP may re-deliver files, Kafka consumers may reprocess on rebalance","Do not couple the Analytics Adapter to internal database queries — it should consume only from the event bus"]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":88,"high_confidence_areas":["Webhook Gateway pattern for 500ms SLA — this is a well-established approach","ACL per external system — textbook DDD integration pattern","Kafka as event backbone given the analytics real-time streaming requirement","Adapter-per-system isolation for independent evolvability"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact Kafka topic partitioning strategy — depends on event volume and ordering requirements not specified","Whether SAP integration is truly unidirectional or bidirectional — assumed primarily inbound (OrderConfirmed) with possible outbound","Analytics platform's exact schema requirements — may need additional transformation complexity"],"assumptions":["The logistics platform already has or will have core Order Management and Shipment Management services that originate the domain events","Kafka infrastructure is available or can be provisioned","DHL webhook retries on failure (standard for major carrier APIs)","SAP delivers one XML file per polling interval with potentially multiple orders","Analytics Platform's Kafka cluster is separate from the internal one"]}}

The design prioritizes loose coupling, independent evolvability of each integration, and operational re

content.thinking: Design an integration architecture for a logistics platform with three external systems.

content.text: {"executive_summary":"Integration architecture for a logistics platform connecting three external systems (SAP ERP via SFTP/XML, DHL Carrier via REST/webhooks, Analytics via Kafka) using an Anti-Corruption Layer per external system, an internal event backbone on Kafka, and dedicated adapter components that translate between external protocols/formats and the platform's canonical domain events. The design prioritizes loose coupling, independent evolvability of each integration, and operational resilience through idempotent processing and dead-letter handling.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Internal Event Bus (Kafka)","responsibility":"Canonical backbone for all domain events (OrderPlaced, OrderConfirmed, ShipmentCreated, DeliveryAttempted, DeliveryCompleted, DeliveryFailed). Single source of truth for event flow. All internal services publish and subscribe here.","boundaries":"Owns the canonical event schema (Avro/Protobuf with a Schema Registry). No external system format leaks into these topics."},{"name":"SAP ERP Adapter (System A)","responsibility":"Polls SFTP endpoint every 15 minutes, retrieves XML files, parses and validates them, translates confirmed orders into internal OrderConfirmed events, and publishes to the Internal Event Bus. Also consumes OrderPlaced events and generates outbound XML files for SAP if bidirectional sync is needed.","boundaries":"Owns the SFTP connection, XML parsing, and SAP-specific data mapping. Contains all SAP schema knowledge. Nothing outside this adapter knows about XML or SFTP."},{"name":"DHL Carrier Adapter (System B)","responsibility":"Exposes a webhook endpoint for DHL shipment status callbacks (DeliveryAttempted, DeliveryCompleted, DeliveryFailed). Also provides an outbound REST client to create shipments with DHL (translates ShipmentCreated intent into DHL API calls). Webhook endpoint must acknowledge within 500ms.","boundaries":"Owns DHL API contract knowledge, authentication, rate limiting, and retry logic. Translates DHL-specific status codes into canonical domain events."},{"name":"Analytics Stream Adapter (System C)","responsibility":"Consumes all domain events from the Internal Event Bus and forwards them to the Analytics Platform's Kafka cluster (or a dedicated set of topics shaped for analytics consumption). Handles schema transformation if the analytics platform expects a different event shape.","boundaries":"Owns the mapping from internal canonical events to the analytics-expected format. Acts as a Kafka-to-Kafka bridge with optional enrichment or filtering."},{"name":"Webhook Gateway","responsibility":"Lightweight HTTP reverse proxy in front of the DHL Carrier Adapter. Receives inbound webhooks, performs signature verification, writes the raw payload to a fast-ingest Kafka topic (dhl.webhooks.raw), and returns 200 immediately. This decouples HTTP response time from processing time, guaranteeing the 500ms SLA.","boundaries":"No business logic. Only authentication, payload capture, and fast acknowledgment."},{"name":"Outbox/Scheduler Service","responsibility":"Manages the SFTP polling schedule for SAP (cron-triggered every 15 min) and handles the transactional outbox pattern for outbound integrations to ensure exactly-once delivery semantics between the internal database and the event bus.","boundaries":"Owns scheduling and delivery guarantees. Does not own business logic."},{"name":"Dead Letter Queue (DLQ) Handler","responsibility":"Captures events that fail processing in any adapter after exhausting retries. Provides alerting, manual replay, and inspection tooling.","boundaries":"Cross-cutting operational concern. Each adapter routes its failures here."}],"interfaces":[{"from":"SAP ERP Adapter","to":"Internal Event Bus (Kafka)","contract":"Publishes OrderConfirmed events in canonical Avro schema to topic 'orders.confirmed'","coupling_type":"asynchronous, event-driven, loose"},{"from":"Internal Event Bus (Kafka)","to":"SAP ERP Adapter","contract":"Adapter subscribes to 'orders.placed' topic if bidirectional sync is required; generates XML and pushes to SFTP","coupling_type":"asynchronous, event-driven, loose"},{"from":"Webhook Gateway","to":"DHL Carrier Adapter","contract":"Raw DHL webhook payloads published to 'dhl.webhooks.raw' Kafka topic; adapter consumes, validates, and translates","coupling_type":"asynchronous, event-driven, loose"},{"from":"DHL Carrier Adapter","to":"Internal Event Bus (Kafka)","contract":"Publishes DeliveryAttempted, DeliveryCompleted, DeliveryFailed events in canonical schema","coupling_type":"asynchronous, event-driven, loose"},{"from":"Internal Event Bus (Kafka)","to":"DHL Carrier Adapter","contract":"Adapter consumes ShipmentCreated events and makes outbound REST calls to DHL's Create Shipment API","coupling_type":"asynchronous consumption triggers synchronous outbound call"},{"from":"Internal Event Bus (Kafka)","to":"Analytics Stream Adapter","contract":"Adapter consumes ALL domain event topics (orders.*, shipments.*, deliveries.*) and produces to analytics-bound topics","coupling_type":"asynchronous, event-driven, loose"},{"from":"Analytics Stream Adapter","to":"Analytics Platform (Kafka)","contract":"Produces transformed events to external Kafka cluster topics per analytics schema contract","coupling_type":"asynchronous, schema-coupled to analytics team's published language"},{"from":"All Adapters","to":"Dead Letter Queue (DLQ) Handler","contract":"Failed events routed to 'dlq.<adapter-name>' topics after retry exhaustion, with original event, error metadata, and attempt count","coupling_type":"asynchronous, operational"}],"data_flow":"1. OrderPlaced originates in the core platform → published to Internal Event Bus. 2. SAP Adapter polls SFTP every 15min → retrieves XML → translates to OrderConfirmed → publishes to bus. 3. Core platform consumes OrderConfirmed → creates shipment → publishes ShipmentCreated. 4. DHL Adapter consumes ShipmentCreated → calls DHL REST API to create shipment → stores tracking ID. 5. DHL sends webhook callbacks → Webhook Gateway acknowledges in <500ms and writes raw payload to Kafka → DHL Adapter consumes raw payload → translates to DeliveryAttempted/DeliveryCompleted/DeliveryFailed → publishes to bus. 6. Analytics Adapter continuously consumes ALL events from bus → transforms → produces to external Analytics Kafka cluster in real-time. 7. Any processing failure after retries → DLQ for manual intervention.","patterns_used":[{"pattern":"Anti-Corruption Layer (ACL)","rationale":"Each external system adapter acts as an ACL, preventing SAP XML schemas, DHL API contracts, and analytics event shapes from polluting the internal canonical domain model. External systems evolve independently; only the relevant adapter changes."},{"pattern":"Event-Driven Architecture / Event Backbone","rationale":"Kafka as the internal event bus decouples all components temporally and spatially. Producers and consumers evolve independently. Enables replay, auditing, and adding new consumers without modifying existing ones."},{"pattern":"Webhook Gateway with Fast-Ingest Queue","rationale":"Decouples the 500ms webhook SLA from actual business processing. The gateway writes raw payloads to Kafka and responds immediately, ensuring DHL never times out regardless of downstream processing latency."},{"pattern":"Transactional Outbox","rationale":"Ensures that database state changes and event publications are atomic. Prevents lost events or ghost events when the platform crashes between committing a transaction and publishing an event."},{"pattern":"Dead Letter Queue","rationale":"Provides operational resilience. Failed events are not lost; they accumulate in DLQ topics with full context for debugging and manual replay."},{"pattern":"Schema Registry (Avro/Protobuf)","rationale":"Enforces a canonical event contract across all producers and consumers. Schema evolution rules (backward/forward compatibility) prevent breaking changes from propagating."},{"pattern":"Adapter Pattern","rationale":"Each external system integration is encapsulated in a dedicated adapter with a clear interface, making integrations independently deployable, testable, and replaceable."},{"pattern":"Polling Consumer (SAP)","rationale":"SAP delivers files via SFTP on a schedule. A polling consumer with idempotent file processing (tracking processed filenames/checksums) is the natural fit for batch file-based integration."}]},"domain_model":{"bounded_contexts":[{"name":"Order Management","classification":"core","aggregates":["Order (root: OrderId, states: Placed → Confirmed → Shipped → Delivered/Failed)"]},{"name":"Shipment Management","classification":"core","aggregates":["Shipment (root: ShipmentId, linked to OrderId, states: Created → InTransit → Delivered/Failed, contains carrier tracking reference)"]},{"name":"ERP Integration","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["ERPSyncRecord (tracks processed SFTP files, maps SAP order IDs to internal OrderIds, idempotency keys)"]},{"name":"Carrier Integration","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["CarrierRequest (tracks outbound shipment creation calls, webhook correlation IDs, delivery attempt history)"]},{"name":"Analytics Integration","classification":"generic","aggregates":["No domain aggregates — stateless stream transformation only"]}],"context_map":[{"upstream":"Order Management","downstream":"ERP Integration","relationship":"Customer-Supplier: Order Management defines the OrderConfirmed event schema; ERP Integration adapter conforms to it and translates from SAP XML."},{"upstream":"Shipment Management","downstream":"Carrier Integration","relationship":"Customer-Supplier: Shipment Management defines ShipmentCreated; Carrier Integration conforms and translates to/from DHL API."},{"upstream":"Order Management + Shipment Management","downstream":"Analytics Integration","relationship":"Conformist: Analytics adapter consumes canonical events as-is and reshapes for the external analytics platform. No influence on upstream schemas."},{"upstream":"SAP ERP (External)","downstream":"ERP Integration","relationship":"Anti-Corruption Layer: ERP Integration translates SAP's XML schema into the internal canonical model, shielding the core domain."},{"upstream":"DHL API (External)","downstream":"Carrier Integration","relationship":"Anti-Corruption Layer: Carrier Integration translates DHL's REST/webhook contracts into canonical domain events."}]},"trade_offs":[{"decision":"Kafka as the internal event backbone (rather than a simpler message broker like RabbitMQ)","gains":"Log-based persistence enables replay and reprocessing. Consumer groups allow independent scaling. Natural fit for the analytics real-time stream requirement. Supports exactly-once semantics with transactions.","sacrifices":"Higher operational complexity. Requires Kafka cluster management, Schema Registry, and topic governance. Overkill if event volume is low.","alternatives_considered":["RabbitMQ with AMQP — simpler to operate but loses replay capability and is a less natural fit for the Kafka-based analytics requirement","Direct point-to-point REST calls between services — tighter coupling, no replay, harder to add new consumers"]},{"decision":"Webhook Gateway as a separate component with fast-ingest Kafka topic","gains":"Guarantees 500ms SLA independent of business logic complexity. Raw payloads are durably stored before processing, so no webhook data is lost even if the DHL adapter is down.","sacrifices":"Additional component to deploy and monitor. Slight increase in end-to-end latency for delivery status updates (gateway → Kafka → adapter → bus). Extra Kafka topic to manage.","alternatives_considered":["Process webhook inline in the adapter — simpler but risks breaching 500ms SLA under load or during downstream issues","Queue in Redis before processing — faster but less durable than Kafka; risks data loss on Redis failure"]},{"decision":"One adapter per external system (rather than a unified integration hub)","gains":"Each adapter is independently deployable, testable, and scalable. Failure in one integration does not affect others. Teams can own individual adapters.","sacrifices":"Some code duplication across adapters (retry logic, DLQ routing, health checks). More deployment units to manage.","alternatives_considered":["Unified ESB/integration hub — single deployment but becomes a monolithic bottleneck and single point of failure","Shared integration library — reduces duplication but creates coupling between adapter deployment cycles"]},{"decision":"Transactional Outbox over direct Kafka publish","gains":"Guarantees consistency between database state and published events. No dual-write problem.","sacrifices":"Requires an outbox table, a polling/CDC mechanism to relay events, and slightly higher publish latency.","alternatives_considered":["Direct publish to Kafka after DB commit — simpler but risks lost events on crash between commit and publish","Change Data Capture (Debezium) on core tables — powerful but adds infrastructure dependency and leaks DB schema into event contracts"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Adding new external system integrations","evolution_path":"New adapters can be added by subscribing to the internal event bus and implementing a new ACL. No changes to existing components. For example, adding a FedEx carrier is a new adapter consuming ShipmentCreated events."},{"area":"Replacing a carrier","evolution_path":"Swap the DHL Carrier Adapter for another carrier's adapter. Internal event contracts remain unchanged. Can run both adapters in parallel during migration using a routing layer."},{"area":"Analytics event enrichment","evolution_path":"The Analytics Stream Adapter can evolve independently — add joins with reference data, filtering, or aggregation — without touching the core domain or other adapters."},{"area":"Event schema evolution","evolution_path":"Schema Registry with backward-compatible evolution rules allows adding fields to domain events without breaking existing consumers. New consumers get richer data; old consumers ignore new fields."},{"area":"Scaling individual integrations","evolution_path":"Each adapter is an independent service with its own Kafka consumer group. The DHL adapter can scale horizontally to handle webhook spikes without affecting SAP or analytics processing."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Canonical event schema","reason":"All components couple to the canonical event definitions (Avro/Protobuf). Changing an event's core structure (renaming fields, changing semantics) requires coordinated updates across producers and consumers. This rigidity is intentional — the canonical model is the system's shared language and must be stable."},{"area":"Kafka as infrastructure","reason":"Deep dependency on Kafka for event backbone, webhook fast-ingest, and analytics streaming. Replacing Kafka would require significant rearchitecture. This is acceptable because Kafka is a proven, scalable technology for this exact use case."},{"area":"SFTP polling interval (15 min)","reason":"Dictated by SAP's file delivery schedule. Cannot get faster order confirmations without SAP changing their integration approach. This is an external constraint, not a design choice."}],"risks":[{"risk":"Webhook Gateway failure causes DHL to stop sending callbacks or mark endpoint as unhealthy","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Deploy Webhook Gateway behind a load balancer with multiple replicas. Implement health checks. DHL typically retries failed webhooks — ensure idempotent processing on our side. Monitor gateway response times with P99 alerting at 400ms."},{"risk":"SAP SFTP file processing fails silently, causing missed OrderConfirmed events","severity":"high","mitigation":"Track processed files by name and checksum in ERPSyncRecord aggregate. Alert if no files received within expected 15-min window (staleness check). Implement file-level idempotency to allow safe reprocessing."},{"risk":"Schema evolution breaks downstream consumers","severity":"high","mitigation":"Enforce backward-compatible schema evolution in Schema Registry. Run compatibility checks in CI/CD pipeline before deploying schema changes. Version topics if breaking changes are unavoidable."},{"risk":"DHL adapter outbound REST calls fail (DHL API downtime), causing ShipmentCreated events to pile up","severity":"high","mitigation":"Implement exponential backoff with circuit breaker on outbound DHL calls. Kafka consumer lag monitoring triggers alerts. DLQ for events that exceed retry budget. Manual replay tooling for recovery."},{"risk":"Kafka consumer lag in Analytics Adapter causes 'real-time' stream to fall behind","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Monitor consumer group lag. Auto-scale Analytics Adapter consumer instances. Set alerting thresholds for acceptable lag (e.g., <30 seconds). If analytics platform Kafka is slow, buffer locally."},{"risk":"Dual-write inconsistency if Transactional Outbox relay fails","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Use a battle-tested outbox relay (Debezium CDC or polling-based). Monitor relay lag. Ensure outbox table is cleaned up periodically to prevent unbounded growth."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Implement each adapter as an independent microservice (or at minimum, independently deployable module). Start with the Webhook Gateway and DHL Adapter first — the 500ms SLA is the tightest constraint and must be validated early. Then implement the SAP Adapter (lower risk due to batch nature), and finally the Analytics Adapter (consumer-only, lowest risk). Use Kafka with Schema Registry from day one — retrofitting schema governance is painful. Use the Transactional Outbox pattern in the core platform services that originate domain events.","files_to_create_or_modify":["services/webhook-gateway/ — Lightweight HTTP service (e.g., Spring Boot / Fastify / Go), receives POST, writes to Kafka, returns 200","services/dhl-adapter/ — Consumes dhl.webhooks.raw and orders.shipment-created topics, calls DHL REST API, publishes delivery events","services/sap-adapter/ — Cron-triggered SFTP poller, XML parser, publishes OrderConfirmed events","services/analytics-adapter/ — Kafka Streams or simple consumer-producer bridge to external analytics Kafka","schemas/ — Avro/Protobuf definitions for all canonical domain events, registered in Schema Registry","infra/kafka/ — Topic definitions, partition strategies, retention policies, consumer group configs","libs/integration-common/ — Shared retry, circuit breaker, DLQ routing, and health check utilities"],"patterns_to_follow":"Each adapter follows the same internal structure: Inbound Port (Kafka consumer or HTTP endpoint) → ACL Translator (maps external format to/from canonical) → Outbound Port (Kafka producer or external API client). Use hexagonal architecture within each adapter. Idempotency keys on all event processing. Circuit breakers on all outbound HTTP calls. Structured logging with correlation IDs across the entire event flow.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["Do not let SAP XML schema types leak into canonical events — always translate through the ACL","Do not process DHL webhooks synchronously in the HTTP handler — always persist first, process second","Do not share a Kafka consumer group across adapters — each adapter needs independent consumption","Do not skip the Schema Registry — ad-hoc JSON events become ungovernable quickly","Do not implement retry logic differently in each adapter — extract to a shared library","Do not forget idempotency — DHL may send duplicate webhooks, SFTP may re-deliver files, Kafka consumers may reprocess on rebalance","Do not couple the Analytics Adapter to internal database queries — it should consume only from the event bus"]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":88,"high_confidence_areas":["Webhook Gateway pattern for 500ms SLA — this is a well-established approach","ACL per external system — textbook DDD integration pattern","Kafka as event backbone given the analytics real-time streaming requirement","Adapter-per-system isolation for independent evolvability"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact Kafka topic partitioning strategy — depends on event volume and ordering requirements not specified","Whether SAP integration is truly unidirectional or bidirectional — assumed primarily inbound (OrderConfirmed) with possible outbound","Analytics platform's exact schema requirements — may need additional transformation complexity"],"assumptions":["The logistics platform already has or will have core Order Management and Shipment Management services that originate the domain events","Kafka infrastructure is available or can be provisioned","DHL webhook retries on failure (standard for major carrier APIs)","SAP delivers one XML file per polling interval with potentially multiple orders","Analytics Platform's Kafka cluster is separate from the internal one"]}}

trace.result: {"executive_summary":"Integration architecture for a logistics platform connecting three external systems (SAP ERP via SFTP/XML, DHL Carrier via REST/webhooks, Analytics via Kafka) using an Anti-Corruption Layer per external system, an internal event backbone on Kafka, and dedicated adapter components that translate between external protocols/formats and the platform's canonical domain events. The design prioritizes loose coupling, independent evolvability of each integration, and operational resilience through idempotent processing and dead-letter handling.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Internal Event Bus (Kafka)","responsibility":"Canonical backbone for all domain events (OrderPlaced, OrderConfirmed, ShipmentCreated, DeliveryAttempted, DeliveryCompleted, DeliveryFailed). Single source of truth for event flow. All internal services publish and subscribe here.","boundaries":"Owns the canonical event schema (Avro/Protobuf with a Schema Registry). No external system format leaks into these topics."},{"name":"SAP ERP Adapter (System A)","responsibility":"Polls SFTP endpoint every 15 minutes, retrieves XML files, parses and validates them, translates confirmed orders into internal OrderConfirmed events, and publishes to the Internal Event Bus. Also consumes OrderPlaced events and generates outbound XML files for SAP if bidirectional sync is needed.","boundaries":"Owns the SFTP connection, XML parsing, and SAP-specific data mapping. Contains all SAP schema knowledge. Nothing outside this adapter knows about XML or SFTP."},{"name":"DHL Carrier Adapter (System B)","responsibility":"Exposes a webhook endpoint for DHL shipment status callbacks (DeliveryAttempted, DeliveryCompleted, DeliveryFailed). Also provides an outbound REST client to create shipments with DHL (translates ShipmentCreated intent into DHL API calls). Webhook endpoint must acknowledge within 500ms.","boundaries":"Owns DHL API contract knowledge, authentication, rate limiting, and retry logic. Translates DHL-specific status codes into canonical domain events."},{"name":"Analytics Stream Adapter (System C)","responsibility":"Consumes all domain events from the Internal Event Bus and forwards them to the Analytics Platform's Kafka cluster (or a dedicated set of topics shaped for analytics consumption). Handles schema transformation if the analytics platform expects a different event shape.","boundaries":"Owns the mapping from internal canonical events to the analytics-expected format. Acts as a Kafka-to-Kafka bridge with optional enrichment or filtering."},{"name":"Webhook Gateway","responsibility":"Lightweight HTTP reverse proxy in front of the DHL Carrier Adapter. Receives inbound webhooks, performs signature verification, writes the raw payload to a fast-ingest Kafka topic (dhl.webhooks.raw), and returns 200 immediately. This decouples HTTP response time from processing time, guaranteeing the 500ms SLA.","boundaries":"No business logic. Only authentication, payload capture, and fast acknowledgment."},{"name":"Outbox/Scheduler Service","responsibility":"Manages the SFTP polling schedule for SAP (cron-triggered every 15 min) and handles the transactional outbox pattern for outbound integrations to ensure exactly-once delivery semantics between the internal database and the event bus.","boundaries":"Owns scheduling and delivery guarantees. Does not own business logic."},{"name":"Dead Letter Queue (DLQ) Handler","responsibility":"Captures events that fail processing in any adapter after exhausting retries. Provides alerting, manual replay, and inspection tooling.","boundaries":"Cross-cutting operational concern. Each adapter routes its failures here."}],"interfaces":[{"from":"SAP ERP Adapter","to":"Internal Event Bus (Kafka)","contract":"Publishes OrderConfirmed events in canonical Avro schema to topic 'orders.confirmed'","coupling_type":"asynchronous, event-driven, loose"},{"from":"Internal Event Bus (Kafka)","to":"SAP ERP Adapter","contract":"Adapter subscribes to 'orders.placed' topic if bidirectional sync is required; generates XML and pushes to SFTP","coupling_type":"asynchronous, event-driven, loose"},{"from":"Webhook Gateway","to":"DHL Carrier Adapter","contract":"Raw DHL webhook payloads published to 'dhl.webhooks.raw' Kafka topic; adapter consumes, validates, and translates","coupling_type":"asynchronous, event-driven, loose"},{"from":"DHL Carrier Adapter","to":"Internal Event Bus (Kafka)","contract":"Publishes DeliveryAttempted, DeliveryCompleted, DeliveryFailed events in canonical schema","coupling_type":"asynchronous, event-driven, loose"},{"from":"Internal Event Bus (Kafka)","to":"DHL Carrier Adapter","contract":"Adapter consumes ShipmentCreated events and makes outbound REST calls to DHL's Create Shipment API","coupling_type":"asynchronous consumption triggers synchronous outbound call"},{"from":"Internal Event Bus (Kafka)","to":"Analytics Stream Adapter","contract":"Adapter consumes ALL domain event topics (orders.*, shipments.*, deliveries.*) and produces to analytics-bound topics","coupling_type":"asynchronous, event-driven, loose"},{"from":"Analytics Stream Adapter","to":"Analytics Platform (Kafka)","contract":"Produces transformed events to external Kafka cluster topics per analytics schema contract","coupling_type":"asynchronous, schema-coupled to analytics team's published language"},{"from":"All Adapters","to":"Dead Letter Queue (DLQ) Handler","contract":"Failed events routed to 'dlq.<adapter-name>' topics after retry exhaustion, with original event, error metadata, and attempt count","coupling_type":"asynchronous, operational"}],"data_flow":"1. OrderPlaced originates in the core platform → published to Internal Event Bus. 2. SAP Adapter polls SFTP every 15min → retrieves XML → translates to OrderConfirmed → publishes to bus. 3. Core platform consumes OrderConfirmed → creates shipment → publishes ShipmentCreated. 4. DHL Adapter consumes ShipmentCreated → calls DHL REST API to create shipment → stores tracking ID. 5. DHL sends webhook callbacks → Webhook Gateway acknowledges in <500ms and writes raw payload to Kafka → DHL Adapter consumes raw payload → translates to DeliveryAttempted/DeliveryCompleted/DeliveryFailed → publishes to bus. 6. Analytics Adapter continuously consumes ALL events from bus → transforms → produces to external Analytics Kafka cluster in real-time. 7. Any processing failure after retries → DLQ for manual intervention.","patterns_used":[{"pattern":"Anti-Corruption Layer (ACL)","rationale":"Each external system adapter acts as an ACL, preventing SAP XML schemas, DHL API contracts, and analytics event shapes from polluting the internal canonical domain model. External systems evolve independently; only the relevant adapter changes."},{"pattern":"Event-Driven Architecture / Event Backbone","rationale":"Kafka as the internal event bus decouples all components temporally and spatially. Producers and consumers evolve independently. Enables replay, auditing, and adding new consumers without modifying existing ones."},{"pattern":"Webhook Gateway with Fast-Ingest Queue","rationale":"Decouples the 500ms webhook SLA from actual business processing. The gateway writes raw payloads to Kafka and responds immediately, ensuring DHL never times out regardless of downstream processing latency."},{"pattern":"Transactional Outbox","rationale":"Ensures that database state changes and event publications are atomic. Prevents lost events or ghost events when the platform crashes between committing a transaction and publishing an event."},{"pattern":"Dead Letter Queue","rationale":"Provides operational resilience. Failed events are not lost; they accumulate in DLQ topics with full context for debugging and manual replay."},{"pattern":"Schema Registry (Avro/Protobuf)","rationale":"Enforces a canonical event contract across all producers and consumers. Schema evolution rules (backward/forward compatibility) prevent breaking changes from propagating."},{"pattern":"Adapter Pattern","rationale":"Each external system integration is encapsulated in a dedicated adapter with a clear interface, making integrations independently deployable, testable, and replaceable."},{"pattern":"Polling Consumer (SAP)","rationale":"SAP delivers files via SFTP on a schedule. A polling consumer with idempotent file processing (tracking processed filenames/checksums) is the natural fit for batch file-based integration."}]},"domain_model":{"bounded_contexts":[{"name":"Order Management","classification":"core","aggregates":["Order (root: OrderId, states: Placed → Confirmed → Shipped → Delivered/Failed)"]},{"name":"Shipment Management","classification":"core","aggregates":["Shipment (root: ShipmentId, linked to OrderId, states: Created → InTransit → Delivered/Failed, contains carrier tracking reference)"]},{"name":"ERP Integration","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["ERPSyncRecord (tracks processed SFTP files, maps SAP order IDs to internal OrderIds, idempotency keys)"]},{"name":"Carrier Integration","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["CarrierRequest (tracks outbound shipment creation calls, webhook correlation IDs, delivery attempt history)"]},{"name":"Analytics Integration","classification":"generic","aggregates":["No domain aggregates — stateless stream transformation only"]}],"context_map":[{"upstream":"Order Management","downstream":"ERP Integration","relationship":"Customer-Supplier: Order Management defines the OrderConfirmed event schema; ERP Integration adapter conforms to it and translates from SAP XML."},{"upstream":"Shipment Management","downstream":"Carrier Integration","relationship":"Customer-Supplier: Shipment Management defines ShipmentCreated; Carrier Integration conforms and translates to/from DHL API."},{"upstream":"Order Management + Shipment Management","downstream":"Analytics Integration","relationship":"Conformist: Analytics adapter consumes canonical events as-is and reshapes for the external analytics platform. No influence on upstream schemas."},{"upstream":"SAP ERP (External)","downstream":"ERP Integration","relationship":"Anti-Corruption Layer: ERP Integration translates SAP's XML schema into the internal canonical model, shielding the core domain."},{"upstream":"DHL API (External)","downstream":"Carrier Integration","relationship":"Anti-Corruption Layer: Carrier Integration translates DHL's REST/webhook contracts into canonical domain events."}]},"trade_offs":[{"decision":"Kafka as the internal event backbone (rather than a simpler message broker like RabbitMQ)","gains":"Log-based persistence enables replay and reprocessing. Consumer groups allow independent scaling. Natural fit for the analytics real-time stream requirement. Supports exactly-once semantics with transactions.","sacrifices":"Higher operational complexity. Requires Kafka cluster management, Schema Registry, and topic governance. Overkill if event volume is low.","alternatives_considered":["RabbitMQ with AMQP — simpler to operate but loses replay capability and is a less natural fit for the Kafka-based analytics requirement","Direct point-to-point REST calls between services — tighter coupling, no replay, harder to add new consumers"]},{"decision":"Webhook Gateway as a separate component with fast-ingest Kafka topic","gains":"Guarantees 500ms SLA independent of business logic complexity. Raw payloads are durably stored before processing, so no webhook data is lost even if the DHL adapter is down.","sacrifices":"Additional component to deploy and monitor. Slight increase in end-to-end latency for delivery status updates (gateway → Kafka → adapter → bus). Extra Kafka topic to manage.","alternatives_considered":["Process webhook inline in the adapter — simpler but risks breaching 500ms SLA under load or during downstream issues","Queue in Redis before processing — faster but less durable than Kafka; risks data loss on Redis failure"]},{"decision":"One adapter per external system (rather than a unified integration hub)","gains":"Each adapter is independently deployable, testable, and scalable. Failure in one integration does not affect others. Teams can own individual adapters.","sacrifices":"Some code duplication across adapters (retry logic, DLQ routing, health checks). More deployment units to manage.","alternatives_considered":["Unified ESB/integration hub — single deployment but becomes a monolithic bottleneck and single point of failure","Shared integration library — reduces duplication but creates coupling between adapter deployment cycles"]},{"decision":"Transactional Outbox over direct Kafka publish","gains":"Guarantees consistency between database state and published events. No dual-write problem.","sacrifices":"Requires an outbox table, a polling/CDC mechanism to relay events, and slightly higher publish latency.","alternatives_considered":["Direct publish to Kafka after DB commit — simpler but risks lost events on crash between commit and publish","Change Data Capture (Debezium) on core tables — powerful but adds infrastructure dependency and leaks DB schema into event contracts"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Adding new external system integrations","evolution_path":"New adapters can be added by subscribing to the internal event bus and implementing a new ACL. No changes to existing components. For example, adding a FedEx carrier is a new adapter consuming ShipmentCreated events."},{"area":"Replacing a carrier","evolution_path":"Swap the DHL Carrier Adapter for another carrier's adapter. Internal event contracts remain unchanged. Can run both adapters in parallel during migration using a routing layer."},{"area":"Analytics event enrichment","evolution_path":"The Analytics Stream Adapter can evolve independently — add joins with reference data, filtering, or aggregation — without touching the core domain or other adapters."},{"area":"Event schema evolution","evolution_path":"Schema Registry with backward-compatible evolution rules allows adding fields to domain events without breaking existing consumers. New consumers get richer data; old consumers ignore new fields."},{"area":"Scaling individual integrations","evolution_path":"Each adapter is an independent service with its own Kafka consumer group. The DHL adapter can scale horizontally to handle webhook spikes without affecting SAP or analytics processing."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Canonical event schema","reason":"All components couple to the canonical event definitions (Avro/Protobuf). Changing an event's core structure (renaming fields, changing semantics) requires coordinated updates across producers and consumers. This rigidity is intentional — the canonical model is the system's shared language and must be stable."},{"area":"Kafka as infrastructure","reason":"Deep dependency on Kafka for event backbone, webhook fast-ingest, and analytics streaming. Replacing Kafka would require significant rearchitecture. This is acceptable because Kafka is a proven, scalable technology for this exact use case."},{"area":"SFTP polling interval (15 min)","reason":"Dictated by SAP's file delivery schedule. Cannot get faster order confirmations without SAP changing their integration approach. This is an external constraint, not a design choice."}],"risks":[{"risk":"Webhook Gateway failure causes DHL to stop sending callbacks or mark endpoint as unhealthy","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Deploy Webhook Gateway behind a load balancer with multiple replicas. Implement health checks. DHL typically retries failed webhooks — ensure idempotent processing on our side. Monitor gateway response times with P99 alerting at 400ms."},{"risk":"SAP SFTP file processing fails silently, causing missed OrderConfirmed events","severity":"high","mitigation":"Track processed files by name and checksum in ERPSyncRecord aggregate. Alert if no files received within expected 15-min window (staleness check). Implement file-level idempotency to allow safe reprocessing."},{"risk":"Schema evolution breaks downstream consumers","severity":"high","mitigation":"Enforce backward-compatible schema evolution in Schema Registry. Run compatibility checks in CI/CD pipeline before deploying schema changes. Version topics if breaking changes are unavoidable."},{"risk":"DHL adapter outbound REST calls fail (DHL API downtime), causing ShipmentCreated events to pile up","severity":"high","mitigation":"Implement exponential backoff with circuit breaker on outbound DHL calls. Kafka consumer lag monitoring triggers alerts. DLQ for events that exceed retry budget. Manual replay tooling for recovery."},{"risk":"Kafka consumer lag in Analytics Adapter causes 'real-time' stream to fall behind","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Monitor consumer group lag. Auto-scale Analytics Adapter consumer instances. Set alerting thresholds for acceptable lag (e.g., <30 seconds). If analytics platform Kafka is slow, buffer locally."},{"risk":"Dual-write inconsistency if Transactional Outbox relay fails","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Use a battle-tested outbox relay (Debezium CDC or polling-based). Monitor relay lag. Ensure outbox table is cleaned up periodically to prevent unbounded growth."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Implement each adapter as an independent microservice (or at minimum, independently deployable module). Start with the Webhook Gateway and DHL Adapter first — the 500ms SLA is the tightest constraint and must be validated early. Then implement the SAP Adapter (lower risk due to batch nature), and finally the Analytics Adapter (consumer-only, lowest risk). Use Kafka with Schema Registry from day one — retrofitting schema governance is painful. Use the Transactional Outbox pattern in the core platform services that originate domain events.","files_to_create_or_modify":["services/webhook-gateway/ — Lightweight HTTP service (e.g., Spring Boot / Fastify / Go), receives POST, writes to Kafka, returns 200","services/dhl-adapter/ — Consumes dhl.webhooks.raw and orders.shipment-created topics, calls DHL REST API, publishes delivery events","services/sap-adapter/ — Cron-triggered SFTP poller, XML parser, publishes OrderConfirmed events","services/analytics-adapter/ — Kafka Streams or simple consumer-producer bridge to external analytics Kafka","schemas/ — Avro/Protobuf definitions for all canonical domain events, registered in Schema Registry","infra/kafka/ — Topic definitions, partition strategies, retention policies, consumer group configs","libs/integration-common/ — Shared retry, circuit breaker, DLQ routing, and health check utilities"],"patterns_to_follow":"Each adapter follows the same internal structure: Inbound Port (Kafka consumer or HTTP endpoint) → ACL Translator (maps external format to/from canonical) → Outbound Port (Kafka producer or external API client). Use hexagonal architecture within each adapter. Idempotency keys on all event processing. Circuit breakers on all outbound HTTP calls. Structured logging with correlation IDs across the entire event flow.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["Do not let SAP XML schema types leak into canonical events — always translate through the ACL","Do not process DHL webhooks synchronously in the HTTP handler — always persist first, process second","Do not share a Kafka consumer group across adapters — each adapter needs independent consumption","Do not skip the Schema Registry — ad-hoc JSON events become ungovernable quickly","Do not implement retry logic differently in each adapter — extract to a shared library","Do not forget idempotency — DHL may send duplicate webhooks, SFTP may re-deliver files, Kafka consumers may reprocess on rebalance","Do not couple the Analytics Adapter to internal database queries — it should consume only from the event bus"]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":88,"high_confidence_areas":["Webhook Gateway pattern for 500ms SLA — this is a well-established approach","ACL per external system — textbook DDD integration pattern","Kafka as event backbone given the analytics real-time streaming requirement","Adapter-per-system isolation for independent evolvability"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact Kafka topic partitioning strategy — depends on event volume and ordering requirements not specified","Whether SAP integration is truly unidirectional or bidirectional — assumed primarily inbound (OrderConfirmed) with possible outbound","Analytics platform's exact schema requirements — may need additional transformation complexity"],"assumptions":["The logistics platform already has or will have core Order Management and Shipment Management services that originate the domain events","Kafka infrastructure is available or can be provisioned","DHL webhook retries on failure (standard for major carrier APIs)","SAP delivers one XML file per polling interval with potentially multiple orders","Analytics Platform's Kafka cluster is separate from the internal one"]}}
